attrs.alt: Entra Permissions Management remediation paths decision flow

content.text: Remediation Paths in Entra Permissions Management

Once EPM identifies over-privileged identities via the Permission Creep Index, there are three remediation paths: right-size to used permissions (generate and deploy a least-privilege policy), remove all standing access and use Permission on Demand (POD) for future requests, or selectively remove unused role assignments while keeping used ones. The right path depends on whether the identity needs ongoing access and how frequently it acts.
Remediation Path
When to Use
Result
Right-size to used permissions
Identity has regular predictable usage
Custom role/policy with only 90-day used actions; large PCI reduction
Full revoke + POD
Identity has rare or unpredictable access needs
No standing permissions; JIT access granted per task and auto-revoked
Remove unused role assignments
Identity needs some standing access but has extra grants
Unused roles removed; core access preserved; moderate PCI reduction
Right-Sizing an Azure Identity

EPM generates a custom Azure role definition JSON containing only the actions the identity actually exercised in the last 90 days. Deploy the custom role, assign it at the narrowest scope needed, then remove the over-privileged assignment.

# Right-size an Azure identity to its used permissions
# EPM generates a custom role definition from 90-day usage data

# Step 1: In EPM portal -> Remediation -> Users -> select high-PCI user -> 'Right-size'
# EPM outputs a JSON custom role definition containing only used actions

# Step 2: Deploy the EPM-generated custom role via PowerShell
$roleDef = Get-Content 'epm-generated-role.json' | ConvertFrom-Json

New-AzRoleDefinition -InputFile 'epm-generated-role.json'

# Step 3: Assign the new least-privilege role to the user
$user  = Get-AzADUser -UserPrincipalName 'alice@contoso.com'
$scope = '/subscriptions/<sub-id>/resourceGroups/<rg>'

New-AzRoleAssignment `
  -ObjectId $user.Id `
  -RoleDefinitionName $roleDef.Name `
  -Scope $scope

# Step 4: Remove the original over-privileged assignment
Remove-AzRoleAssignment `
  -ObjectId $user.Id `
  -RoleDefinitionName 'Contributor' `
  -Scope $scope

Write-Host "Right-sizing complete. Verify PCI in EPM dashboard within 24h."
Right-Sizing an AWS Identity
For AWS, EPM generates a least-privilege IAM policy from CloudTrail data. Create the managed policy, attach it to the user or role, then detach the over-privileged managed policy.

# Right-size an AWS IAM user to used permissions (EPM-generated policy)

# Step 1: EPM generates a least-privilege IAM policy JSON from CloudTrail usage
$leastPrivPolicy = @'
{
  "Version": "2012-10-17",
  "Statement": [
    {
      "Effect": "Allow",
      "Action": [
        "s3:GetObject",
        "s3:ListBucket",
        "ec2:DescribeInstances"
      ],
      "Resource": "*"
    }
  ]
}
'@

# Step 2: Create the managed policy in AWS
aws iam create-policy `
  --policy-name 'EPM-LeastPrivilege-alice' `
  --policy-document $leastPrivPolicy

# Step 3: Attach least-privilege policy and detach over-privileged policy
aws iam attach-user-policy `
  --user-name alice `
  --policy-arn 'arn:aws:iam::<account>:policy/EPM-LeastPrivilege-alice'

aws iam detach-user-policy `
  --user-name alice `
  --policy-arn 'arn:aws:iam::aws:policy/AmazonS3FullAccess'
Right-Sizing a GCP Service Account
EPM generates a GCP custom role YAML from Cloud Audit Log usage. Create the custom role in GCP, bind it to the service account, then remove the over-privileged primitive role (Viewer, Editor, Owner).

# Right-size a GCP service account using EPM-generated custom role
# EPM generates a YAML custom role definition from Cloud Audit Log usage data

# EPM output example (save as epm-custom-role.yaml):
# title: EPM-LeastPrivilege-deploy-sa
# description: Auto-generated least-privilege role for deploy-sa@project.iam.gserviceaccount.com
# stage: GA
# includedPermissions:
#   - storage.objects.get
#   - storage.buckets.list
#   - run.services.get

# Step 1: Create the custom role in GCP
gcloud iam roles create EPMLeastPrivilegeDeploySA `
  --project=my-project `
  --file=epm-custom-role.yaml

# Step 2: Bind the custom role to the service account
gcloud projects add-iam-policy-binding my-project `
  --member='serviceAccount:deploy-sa@my-project.iam.gserviceaccount.com' `
  --role='projects/my-project/roles/EPMLeastPrivilegeDeploySA'

# Step 3: Remove the over-privileged binding
gcloud projects remove-iam-policy-binding my-project `
  --member='serviceAccount:deploy-sa@my-project.iam.gserviceaccount.com' `
  --role='roles/editor'
Permission on Demand (POD) Workflow

POD replaces standing elevated access with task-scoped, time-bound grants. The user requests specific actions with a justification; an approver reviews; EPM creates the temporary assignment and revokes it automatically when the window expires.

POD is the right pattern for privileged actions that are needed rarely — cloud admin operations, data migrations, incident response. It eliminates standing over-privilege while preserving the ability to perform the action when legitimately needed.
Bulk Revocation of Completely Inactive Identities
Identities with PCI = 100 have had zero cloud activity for 90 days. After a manager-review cycle, run a scripted bulk revocation to remove all standing assignments from these identities.

# Bulk revoke unused Azure role assignments for all identities with PCI = 100
# Run after manager-approval review cycle

Connect-MgGraph -Scopes 'PermissionsManagement.Read.All'
Connect-AzAccount

$azureSysId = '<azure-authorization-system-id>'

# Get all completely inactive users (PCI 100)
$inactive = Invoke-MgGraphRequest -Method GET `
  -Uri "https://graph.microsoft.com/beta/external/authorizationSystems/$azureSysId/microsoft.graph.azureAuthorizationSystem/associatedIdentities/users?`$filter=permissionsCreepIndex/score eq 100" |
  Select-Object -ExpandProperty value

foreach ($user in $inactive) {
    $upn = $user.userPrincipalName
    Write-Host "Processing: $upn (PCI 100)"

    $assignments = Get-AzRoleAssignment -SignInName $upn -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
    if (-not $assignments) {
        Write-Host "  No Azure RBAC assignments found"
        continue
    }

    foreach ($a in $assignments) {
        Write-Host "  Removing: $($a.RoleDefinitionName) at $($a.Scope)"
        Remove-AzRoleAssignment -InputObject $a -Confirm:$false -ErrorAction Continue
    }
}
Write-Host "Bulk revocation complete for $($inactive.Count) inactive identities"
Auditing Remediation Activity via KQL
EPM logs every remediation action to Entra Audit Logs. Query these to verify right-sizing completed and to build a compliance evidence trail.

// Track all EPM remediation events — verify right-sizing completed successfully
AuditLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(30d)
| where LoggedByService == 'Permissions Management'
| where OperationName has_any('Revoke', 'Right-size', 'Remove', 'Grant')
| extend Actor        = tostring(InitiatedBy.app.displayName ?? InitiatedBy.user.userPrincipalName)
| extend Target       = tostring(TargetResources[0].displayName)
| extend CloudSystem  = tostring(AdditionalDetails[?(@.key == 'authorizationSystem')].value)
| project TimeGenerated, OperationName, Actor, Target, CloudSystem, Result
| order by TimeGenerated desc
Remediation Cadence Recommendations
Frequency
Activity
Focus
Weekly
Review new High-PCI identities (PCI >= 67 this week)
Newly over-provisioned identities
Monthly
Bulk right-size all PCI = 100 identities (after approval)
Completely inactive standing permissions
Quarterly
Re-generate custom roles for right-sized identities
Usage drift — catch permissions that became unused since last right-sizing
Continuously
POD for privileged operations
Prevent new standing over-privilege from being created
Automated Remediation via Logic Apps

EPM integrates with Azure Logic Apps to trigger automated right-sizing workflows when PCI thresholds are breached. The Logic App polls Graph API daily for high-PCI identities and initiates the appropriate remediation path.
Verifying Remediation Effectiveness

After revoking or right-sizing permissions, verify the PCI score has decreased and the identity no longer appears in the high-risk list. Allow 24-48 hours for EPM to rescan and recalculate scores before measuring the outcome.

# Compare PCI before and after remediation
function Get-IdentityPCI {
    param([string]$IdentityId, [string]$Cloud = 'azure')
    $uri = 'https://graph.microsoft.com/beta/identityGovernance/permissionsManagement/' +
           "permissionsAnalytics/$Cloud/findings/" +
           'microsoft.graph.permissionsAnalyticsAggregation?' +
           "`$filter=identity/id eq '$IdentityId'"
    $r = Invoke-MgGraphRequest -Method GET -Uri $uri
    $r.value[0].permissionsCreepIndex
}

$baselinePCI = (Get-IdentityPCI -IdentityId 'svc-pipeline' -Cloud 'azure').index
Write-Host "Baseline PCI: $baselinePCI"

# Wait 24h for EPM rescan, then verify
$postPCI = (Get-IdentityPCI -IdentityId 'svc-pipeline' -Cloud 'azure').index
Write-Host "Post-remediation PCI: $postPCI"

$reduction = $baselinePCI - $postPCI
Write-Host "PCI reduction: $reduction points"
if ($reduction -lt 20) {
    Write-Warning 'Expected reduction not achieved - verify all assignments were removed'
}
Drift Detection and Re-Creep Prevention

Permissions re-creep occurs when automation or manual grants restore over-privileged access after remediation. Use a Sentinel Watchlist of previously remediated identities and alert when their PCI climbs above 70 again.
Remediation Exceptions and Compensating Controls

Some identities legitimately require broad permissions — break-glass accounts, platform engineering service principals. Document time-limited exceptions with compensating controls rather than leaving the PCI unaddressed.
Control Type
Implementation
Review Cadence
Time-limited exception
PIM eligible assignment, max 8h activation
Monthly
Monitoring intensification
Sentinel alert on every sign-in action
Per-alert
Network restriction
Conditional Access location and device policy
Quarterly
Approval gate
POD workflow for any privilege use above read
Per-request
Peer review
All grants require second engineer approval
Per-grant

# Register a time-limited remediation exception for a break-glass account
$body = @{
    identityId        = 'svc-break-glass@contoso.com'
    justification     = 'Break-glass: requires Global Admin for DR scenarios'
    exceptionCategory = 'BreakGlass'
    expiryDate        = (Get-Date).AddDays(90).ToString('o')
    approvedBy        = 'ciso@contoso.com'
    compensatingControls = @(
        'Sentinel alert on every sign-in'
        'MFA enforced via Conditional Access'
        'Password rotated monthly via PAM'
    )
} | ConvertTo-Json -Depth 3

Invoke-MgGraphRequest \
    -Method POST \
    -Uri 'https://graph.microsoft.com/beta/identityGovernance/permissionsManagement/exceptions' \
    -Body $body \
    -ContentType 'application/json'
Cross-Cloud Remediation Coordination

In multi-cloud environments the same federated identity often holds high-PCI roles in Azure, AWS, and GCP simultaneously. Use EPM's unified view to coordinate remediation across all three clouds before closing the remediation ticket.
Summary
EPM remediation has three paths: right-size to used permissions (deploy least-privilege policy), full revoke + POD (no standing access, JIT grants), or selective removal of unused role assignments.
Right-sizing uses EPM-generated custom role/policy JSON built from 90-day CloudTrail/Activity Log/Cloud Audit Log data — deploy the custom role and remove the over-privileged assignment.
Permission on Demand eliminates standing privileged access: the user requests the specific actions needed for a task, an approver grants them for a time window, and EPM revokes them automatically.
Bulk revocation of PCI = 100 identities (completely inactive) is the fastest way to achieve a large PCI reduction — run after a manager-approval review cycle.
Audit all remediation events via Entra Audit Logs (LoggedByService == 'Permissions Management') to build a compliance evidence trail and verify right-sizing completed successfully.
Set a remediation cadence: weekly triage of new High-PCI identities, monthly bulk revocation of PCI 100 accounts, quarterly re-generation of custom roles to catch usage drift.
